title: Crispy Hunan Eggplant and Tofu
intro: Hunan cooking is bold, direct, and unapologetically spicy. Fresh chiles, garlic, and vinegar drive the flavor, while high heat and fast wok work build intensity and depth. This dish highlights that spirit with crisp-edged eggplant and tofu glazed in a fiery chili-garlic sauce. The focus is texture and heat: a lacquered exterior, tender centers, and a sauce that clings rather than overwhelms.

items.prep: enough for ¼ inch depth in wok

stepGroups.title: Prepare the Eggplant
items: Toss the eggplant cubes with 1 teaspoon kosher salt. Spread on a wire rack set over a sheet pan and let sit 30 to 45 minutes. Pat thoroughly dry. Do not rinse.

stepGroups.title: Press and Dry the Tofu
items: Wrap the tofu in a clean towel and place on a rimmed sheet pan or cutting board. Set a second sheet pan or flat plate on top and weigh it down with a cast iron skillet or canned goods.
Press for 25 minutes, flipping halfway through.
Cut into 1-inch cubes and place uncovered on a wire rack for 15 minutes to dry the surface.

stepGroups.title: Dredge
items: Combine cornstarch, 1/2 teaspoon kosher salt, and black pepper in a shallow dish.
Toss eggplant to coat evenly. Shake off excess. Repeat with tofu. Let rest on rack 5 minutes.

stepGroups.title: Shallow Fry in the Wok
items: Heat avocado oil in a wok to a depth of about ¼ inch over medium-high heat until shimmering.
Fry the eggplant first, working in batches. Turn gently and cook until deeply golden and crisp on all sides, about 2 to 3 minutes per side. Transfer to a wire rack.
Fry the tofu the same way until golden and crisp. Drain on the rack. Do not use paper towels, which soften the crust.

stepGroups.title: Build the Sauce
items: In a bowl, whisk together the stock, soy sauce, rice vinegar, lime juice, and cornstarch until smooth.
Carefully pour off the frying oil and wipe out the wok.
Return the wok to medium heat and add a thin film of fresh avocado oil. Add the ginger, garlic, and minced chiles. Stir constantly for about 30 seconds until fragrant but not browned.
Stir the sauce mixture again to re-suspend the cornstarch, then pour it into the wok. Bring to a simmer and cook 2 to 3 minutes until glossy and lightly thickened.

stepGroups.title: Glaze, Do Not Soak
items: Return the eggplant and tofu to the wok. Toss gently just until coated. The sauce should cling lightly and leave the crust intact. Remove from heat and stir in the toasted sesame oil.

stepGroups.title: Serve Immediately
items: Serve over brown jasmine rice and finish with thinly sliced raw scallions. Eat while the exterior is still crisp and the interior te
